disease_info.description: Viral skin infections are caused by viruses such as herpes simplex, varicella-zoster, or molluscum contagiosum. They produce vesicles, blisters, grouped sores, or dome-shaped lesions.
disease_info.common_symptoms: Fluid-filled blisters, grouped vesicles, burning sensation, fever (sometimes)
disease_info.recommended_action: Maintain hygiene, avoid touching lesions, antiviral therapy may be required for herpes or shingles.
disease_info.image_indicators: Clustered vesicles, blisters, red base, central depression
